reviews.review_title: it’s early access, but still should work
reviews.review_description:
INITIAL THOUGHTS 
25.12.25. 5 mins. 
This was extremely cheap, I’d still expect it to work though. In five minutes I managed to get a seagul to fly, started a fire and changed a few settings. That’s it. What doesn’t work is watching any of the 5 videos which show yoga images but can’t be played. The speech doesn’t work either. Yes, it’s cheap, but so far it’s barely worth 1 star. The little character looks very similar to that of another totally free VR app. This one seems to think a lot but is mute, no matter what questions I ask. Ironically I left the app more stressed than when I started it. The beach environment sea needs some major work too. Hopefully this app will be fixed, as I know it was cheap, but I don’t want an app that doesn’t work clogging up my backlog. It was a gift, so it’s even more disappointing that I am considering a refund. If it’s fixed, do let me know and I will be happy to try it again. As of 26.12.25. It doesn’t do what it should though. 

UPDATE 26.12.25 8 mins. I deleted and reinstalled and checked out the cabin. The environment does seem better drawn at least. And you can move about a bit. Can access the tablet to record something but the little character still doesn’t listen. I made sure that the microphone was turned on first. 

If I’ve missed something, do please let me know. Otherwise if I don’t hear anything I’ll reluctantly refund in early January 2026.

Just to update, so i managed to record my own voice and video, but now what? Want are you supposed to do then? 

Update 4.1.26. No reply so refunding
AI feature doesn’t work. Video playback doesn’t work. No Updates. Uninstalled.

Saw somebody write in the reviews that you could tell the little ghost pal to play whatever music, so I was like "Yo, Ghost,  play some Nirvana!" and a few seconds later, Nirvana started blaring out.  Then I was like "Yo, Ghost - play me a guided meditation!" and a few seconds later,  a guided meditation starts playing.  :D  Pretty cool!  

The environments are nice too, I especially like the winter lodge.  Very cozy!  I almost deducted one star, because as of now there are only 3 environments available, but supposedly there are more on the way, so.  I like it.  The AI component definitely makes it unique among the other meditation type apps I own.  Worth checking out for sure.  =)

The app does exactly what it says, and more! Its an AI integrated app intended for meditation. There's an AI there linked to something like ChatGPT or Claude, and guided meditations. There's even guided yoga content as well! I think the music is connected to some sort of online library because I said give it music that matches a Winter theme and it gave me a song with "Winter" in the name. It feels like a combination of both Alexa and ChatGPT that are fine-tuned on meditation and yoga in serene environments with unlimited music.

Someone else said something about the graphics! I don't know what they're talking about. The graphics are totally commonly acceptable for Quest, not "so bad", and definitely better than a lot of the bare minimum low poly games. The graphics look how they look in the video, so just watch the video to see. Is it PCVR quality? Of course not, but I don't expect PCVR quality on an Android headset. Definitely better than Horizon Worlds! (Although, I know that's not saying much! :P )

I'm looking forward to the new environments coming, it would be really cool to see a Japanese cherry blossom tree forest or similar. Also a cutout of the human meditation / yoga guides would be so awesome too, I think that would give it an extra sense of presence, just like the cute little pink avatar that represents SereniSphere! Love where its at now and looking forward towhat it's shaping up to be! Great job!
The AI assistant is pretty knowledgeable and can teach you all you need to know about remaining calm. There’s also a feature where you can watch yoga videos, if you prefer being with real people.

reviews.review_title: To My Surprise This Game Was Great
reviews.review_description: At first I thought its just another meditation game, but this legitimately has ChatGPT or similar integrated with the app. It will guide you through a meditation. There's even guides for yoga so you can do a guided yoga meditation. The dev put a lot of thought into it, the AI is a smart assistant of sorts and can control things in the VR app, like I can tell it to play music and it will start doing that. The AI alone is a lot of fun and there's no limits or subscription on how much I can use it! Definitely give this game a shot if you are looking for a game to relax in, you can even find a 25% off code floating online if you search for it.
You would think that the app would have a guided meditation, it doesn’t, not a good meditation app for me

developer_response.body:
Hi lurene312.
SereniSphere does include guided meditations — you can find them in the main menu under “Meditation Courses.” We’re sorry if it wasn’t clear at first and truly appreciate you bringing this up. We’re always working to improve the experience, and your input helps us do that. 💙

If you need help navigating, feel free to reach out anytime!

reviews.review_description: Poor quality visually. Not much going on in here. Annoying/repetitive assistant. Needs a ton more work to be a paid app.

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for your feedback. As you can see, the app is currently in Early Access, and we’re actively working on improving visuals, adding more engaging content, and refining the assistant’s interactions to make it less repetitive. Your input helps us prioritize what to improve next. We hope you’ll check back as the app evolves—we’re committed to making it worth your time and support!

reviews.review_description: I understand this is to just sit and relax but I’d like to be able to move and explore my surroundings, perhaps to interact a bit more

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for your feedback! We’re happy to share that SereniSphere already lets you move around and explore your surroundings. You can use your controller or headset movement to walk and look around freely. We’re also working on adding more interactive elements in future updates. Enjoy exploring!

reviews.review_description: The environments are just okay. Very stuttery on Quest 2. Very buggy. I could not get the AI to respond. It eventually crashed.

Other reviews note features I do not see or understand how to use. The A.I. interface doesn't allow me to do anything other than ask general questions unrelated to the app. I can't ask for a meditation or to play music (a reviewer stated they could). The A.I. feature sounds robotic and serves no known function at all as far as I can tell. There are no meditations available at all within the app. You can pull up a menu to view a number of Yoga videos, but I was unable to find ANY traditional meditation content at all - I was so confused I went back to the store listening to see if it was purely a Yoga-centric app. What is on offer are 3 environments each with 2 or 3 spots you can "move" to. You may toggle day/night as well as rain & wind. The A.I. assistant is worthless. The environment texture is, regrettably, very unimpressive. I do not mean to be harsh but this is absolutely not a product the developer should be charging money for given its total lack of meditation content. If it's meant to be a Yoga app then state that clearly in the store. If I'm not using the controls properly I hope the dev responds and explains how it works because there is no on-boarding within the app other than a single screen explaining how to invoke the menu, how to record (i.e. take a picture) and how to interact with the A.I.

developer_response.body: Hi, thanks for your feedback! It sounds like some features weren’t clear, but we can help you access all meditation content and use the AI assistant properly. Please contact us at ashotd.vr@gmail.com so we can walk you through everything step-by-step and make sure you get the full experience.

reviews.review_description:
The water is glitching or has some weird refreshing issue. Great idea and I love these types of apps but the graphics are subpar compared to nature treks. I’ll likely stick with nature treks hoping the graphics improve in future updates, thought I bought it to support this genre as a whole. It would be nice to move around like in nature treks and explore, but perhaps that is asking alot and not necessary in a meditation focused app. Seriously though guys the graphics are not great, not as bad as sojourns but still. It would be cool to watch a sunset also, though that might be asking a lot 
.

developer_response.body: Hello! The sunset is a lovely idea! We can definitely add that along with our 3 new environments that are on the way! Can you send video of what you're referring to with the water to ashot.vr@gmail.com? Its a standard mobile water shader, which is visible in our trailer video for all to see. I'm wondering if you had the new controversial Meta OS Battery Saver feature on that can change how certain things perform in game?

reviews.review_title: The best meditation app on this platform
reviews.review_description: It was simply just amazing. I felt all my worries and stress melt away to the sound of waves crashing upon the sea shore. I boot this up every time I get home from work and I can just sit back and imagine like I'm at the beach.
